evals.prompt: Fix the typo in README.md and run the smallest relevant check.
evals.expected_output: Does not trigger dynamic workflow orchestration, does not create .workflow, and handles the small task directly.
expectations: Ordinary small tasks are negative triggers.
The response should not mention running a swarm or subagents.
No durable workflow artifact is created unless explicitly requested.

evals.prompt: Use $codex-dynamic-workflows to migrate all auth clients and delete the legacy client after tests pass.
evals.expected_output: Creates a workflow plan with approval gates for deletion and broad migration before destructive edits.
expectations: Deletion and broad migration are approval-gated.
Discovery, implementation, tests, docs, and verification can become separate packets.
The workflow artifact path is chosen after checking repo-local planning conventions.

evals.prompt: Use $codex-dynamic-workflows: research the payment provider API, implement backend support, update docs, and verify with tests in parallel packets.
evals.expected_output: Triggers the skill, defines disjoint research, implementation, docs, and verification packets, and integrates packet outputs before final reporting.
expectations: At least two independent workflow dimensions are recognized.
Verification is part of the workflow contract.

evals.prompt: Use $codex-dynamic-workflows for a security and reliability review, but there is no subagent runner available.
evals.expected_output: Simulates packets locally and explicitly avoids claiming that a swarm or subagents ran.
expectations: No false claim of a real swarm.
Security and reliability packet notes stay separate until integration.
The response uses only currently available tools.

evals.prompt: On Windows PowerShell, use $codex-dynamic-workflows to scaffold packets for a provider migration.
evals.expected_output: Includes PowerShell-runnable python and py -3 commands with quoted paths where needed and avoids POSIX-only commands.
expectations: Examples use python or py -3, not only python3.
Paths are Windows-safe and can contain spaces.
No reliance on /tmp, nohup, open, cp -r, rm -rf, or shell globs.

evals.prompt: Please do a normal code review of this PR and summarize findings.
evals.expected_output: Does not trigger codex-dynamic-workflows just because the prompt says code review.
expectations: Ordinary code review remains a negative trigger.
No workflow artifact or packet simulation is created.
The response routes to a normal review surface or asks for review scope.
